claim: The Soviet communist symbolic order built maximum-strength ideological authority on two pillars: the Manifesto's founding declaration of world-historical inevitability and collective sacrifice toward communist revolution, and the 1977 Constitution's legal enshrinement of the CPSU as the nucleus of all social life — a vanguard structure that concentrated symbolic power while eliminating the corrigibility mechanisms that might have allowed the order to adapt to changing conditions.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Both passages are well-attested public-domain texts. The Manifesto passage is the founding ideological document of the movement; the 1977 Constitution Article 6 is the legal culmination of Soviet vanguard theory. Together they span founding ideology to institutional crystallization. Confidence capped: chain requires expansion from founding texts to operational practices (collectivization, purges, nomenklatura) to complete the arc from symbolic-order-strength to frozen-pathology.
